title: eidetic-cli ships its memory surface: remember ingests records, recall returns top-k cited hits with text plus full provenance metadata plus score

claims.text: eidetic-cli ships its memory surface: remember ingests records, recall returns top-k cited hits with text plus full provenance metadata plus score

honesty_conditions.text: the #3 consumer (jetson-ai-lab-cli) can remember a record and later recall it with full provenance across a subprocess boundary, adding zero eidetic-side dependency to the consumer

claims.text: AI-agent consumers over a subprocess boundary, with no bespoke per-consumer coupling: (a) any AgentCulture mesh or colleague agent, present and future - the #3 jetson-ai-lab-cli read-only Discord and docs agent, the #1 research pipeline stages (arxivist, tensor, reduce, prove), and other current or future mesh agents; and (b) all Claude (Claude Code) agents, which call eidetic as a drop-in replacement for the existing ~/.claude memory skill (core and working notes plus MongoDB RAG plus Neo4j knowledge graph)

honesty_conditions.text: the remember and recall contract is generic enough that any consumer - a mesh or colleague agent, a future agent like arxivist, or a Claude Code agent using eidetic in place of the existing memory skill - can call it over the subprocess boundary with no eidetic-side change per consumer

claims.text: eidetic durably stores text records and serves ranked, cited recall, so the scaffold finally does its declared job of perfect-recall memory

honesty_conditions.text: a remembered record survives across separate CLI invocations (process restarts) and is later retrievable: storage is durable, not in-memory only

claims.text: only the scaffold exists (whoami, learn, explain, overview, doctor); remember and recall are unbuilt; consumers have nowhere durable to store or retrieve

honesty_conditions.text: no existing eidetic command stores or retrieves records today; remember and recall are genuinely absent, not partially present

claims.text: consumers build cited answers, so recall is unusable unless every hit round-trips full provenance metadata plus a score

honesty_conditions.text: a recall hit carries enough provenance (source plus locator or permalink) for the consumer to render a citation without a second lookup

claims.text: eidetic only remembers and retrieves; it does not discover, conjecture, decompose, or prove; not a general database. Data sensitivity is per-namespace: the #3 discord and docs namespace is public-data-only (its own contract), while private namespaces such as Claude-agent memory are local-only and never exposed to public or shared consumers; there is no global public-only rule

honesty_conditions.text: eidetic performs no discovery, conjecture, decomposition, or proving; every scope is labeled public or private and a private scope's records never leak into a public scope's recall; the #3 public scope holds public data only

claims.text: the #3 consumer can batch-remember via NDJSON on stdin and recall with top-k JSON, every hit carrying mandatory metadata plus score; re-ingesting the same id never duplicates

honesty_conditions.text: an end-to-end test shows batch NDJSON ingest then recall top-k json with metadata and score, and a duplicate-id re-ingest leaves the record count unchanged

claims.text: ingest is idempotent upsert by id or hash; recall returns text plus full metadata plus score and honors --json; each new noun group stays rubric-green with overview, learn entry, and explain catalog

honesty_conditions.text: remember the same id twice yields exactly one record, updated in place, proven by a test
recall never returns a hit missing metadata or score; provenance is mandatory

consumers calling eidetic over the subprocess boundary inherit no eidetic dependencies; eidetic itself declares its required store deps (neo4j and pymongo) and reaches embeddings and rerank over HTTP, so the dependency surface never crosses into consumers
teken cli doctor --strict stays green: each new noun group exposes overview, a learn entry, and an explain catalog

claims.text: storage and embedding weight stays behind the eidetic process boundary so consumers stay dependency-free: subprocess, not import
which backing store: zero-dep files, model-gear HTTP embeddings with a local vector store, or data-refinery with neo4j plus mongo plus TEI embeddings

memory storage is a pluggable backend; the files backend is the lightweight default for private and local scopes (no database needed); Neo4j and Mongo are required backends for shared and public scopes, with their store, embedding, and cypher logic adapted from data-refinery (cite-don't-import) rather than depending on its qq_memory database or instance; eidetic declares neo4j and pymongo as dependencies; embeddings and reranking go through model-gear over HTTP

every record belongs to a scope (namespace) labeled public or private; recall is scope-aware and records in a private scope never leak into a public scope recall; private scopes such as Claude-agent memory are local-only; eidetic replaces the existing ~/.claude memory skill, which becomes a thin client over remember and recall

honesty_conditions.text: a recall in a public scope never returns a record from a private scope, proven by an isolation test; and the existing memory skill facts round-trip through eidetic remember and recall

claims.text: data sensitivity is per-scope visibility, public or private, chosen over a global public-only rule and over per-record access control; the load-bearing invariant is no private-to-public leak; public and shared scopes live in the data-refinery store while private scopes are local-only

open_vagueness.text: RESOLVED by decision c11 (pluggable files/neo4j/mongo backend over model-gear embeddings+reranker, data-refinery as backing store). Non-blocking residual: extend data-refinery with an ingest/upsert path and a vector/top-k search, and wire the currently-unused qq_memory mongo
